results.alt: The letters swam before my eyes for weeks. Then one morning, like a door swinging open, the marks on the page became words, and the words became worlds... Learning to Read

My grandmother's hands, brown and weathered like the earth itself, moving through the wheat fields at dawn. She hummed a song her mother taught her, and her mother before that... The Last Harvest

I was seven years old, sitting on my father's shoulders in a crowd of thousands. The screen showed a grainy image and the whole world held its breath together... First Steps on the Moon

We had nothing but what we could carry. The river was cold and fast. My father held my hand so tight I thought my fingers would break, but I never let go... The River Crossing

He wrote it by candlelight, the ink still wet when the messenger came. I have read it ten thousand times. I know every word by heart, yet I still find new meaning... The Last Letter

Three days of music, the smell of saffron and rose water, cousins I hadn't seen in years dancing until their feet ached. My mother cried and laughed at the same time... The Wedding Feast
